_doc: The autonomy framework's EXTERNAL STATE (Lilian Weng 'long-term memory' + 'planning'). The Stop hook (.claude/hooks/autonomy_loop.py) and the driver (scripts/autonomy/autonomy_driver.py) read this. Copy to runs/autonomy/frontier.json and ARM via the AUTHORITATIVE source -- set .claude/autonomous_mode.json autonomous=true (+ envelope_end for a timed run). (Legacy AUTONOMY_ON still works ONLY when autonomous_mode.json is absent.) The OVERSEER (Tier-0, see .claude/skills/_common/OVERSEER.md) owns this file; Tier-1 workers report up but do not own it. The MODEL updates this file each cycle; the LOOP just re-reads it.
objective: <the problem as a verifiable goal, e.g. 'develop a SOL spot system aiming for 1-5% ROI per fire, proven robust (10/10 seeds positive UNSEEN, p05>0, maxDD<30%, beats a cost-matched random-entry null)'>
success_criteria: <how the loop KNOWS it is solved -- the verifiable stop condition, e.g. 'a ship-tier candidate passes the full robustness battery on UNSEEN'>

overseer._doc: Tier-0 user-proxy CONTRACT + judgment ledger -- the 'stand in for the user' record. Filled in cycle 1 (ADOPT + FORM THE FULFILLMENT CONTRACT). Tier-1 workers never write here; the Overseer does.
overseer.adopted_command: <the user's raw command, verbatim -- what was adopted>
overseer.acceptance_test: <the SINGLE command/check that, if it passes, means DONE -- e.g. 'python src/strat/selftest_all.py green AND candidate beats the random-entry null on EVERY window, net of taker cost'>
stop_conditions: SOLVED (acceptance_test VERIFIED by the Overseer, not asserted by a worker)
STRUCTURALLY_IMPOSSIBLE (objective shown infeasible, with the falsifier)
NEEDS_IRREVERSIBLE_OK (deploy real capital / external send / shared-history rewrite -- the only things that escalate to the real user)
overseer.user_proxy_notes: <decisions the Overseer made AS the user, with reasoning -- so the real user can audit/override; the Overseer does NOT block waiting on the user for anything git can revert>

budget.note: spent increments each cycle; loop releases at max_cycles

nodes.task: RED-TEAM n1: is the method/data/apparatus sound? (-k falsifier -- highest-value breadth node)
GENERALIZE: is there a higher-leverage framing / the general class this belongs to? (+k -- anti-narrow-mindedness)

status_legend: node.status in {open, done, refuted, blocked, parked}; kind in {build, verify, diverge, decide}; valence + = opportunity, - = falsifier
